body: Cross-card scope: spec touches both 0005-drive and 0006-rally because the layout migration is one substrate-wide change — splitting it would create a window where drive sidecars are flat and rally sidecars are folder, which complicates resumption and tooling. ac-06 (existing-folder policy) and ac-09 (post-ship integration test) are deferred to implementation time when the actual on-disk state can be inspected.

Engineering finding 2026-05-09: orbit's canonicaliser + verify scan .orbit/specs/*.yaml as specs. The sidecar shape <id>.drive.yaml therefore fails parse ('unknown field spec_id, expected one of id, goal, cards, status, labels, acceptance_criteria'). The migration must include teaching orbit's scanner about sidecars — either (a) skip files matching <id>.<sidecar>.yaml when the prefix is a known spec id, (b) parse them under a separate sidecar schema, or (c) place sidecars in a sub-namespace .orbit/sidecars/<id>.<sidecar>.yaml. This is the crux of the migration: not just changing paths in skill files but extending the substrate scanner. Add an AC covering this when implementing the sidecar spec.

Implementation ordering (post review-spec cycle 1): ac-00 (scanner fix) MUST land first as a single self-contained commit. Without it, ac-01/ac-03/ac-04/ac-05 produce sidecar yaml on disk that bricks orbit verify and orbit spec list. After ac-00, the SKILL.md edits (ac-01/ac-02/ac-03/ac-04/ac-05) can land in any order. ac-08 (canonical doc) and ac-07 (smoke test) can land alongside or after the SKILL.md edits. ac-06 is satisfied by ac-00's filter (existing folder layouts are not at .orbit/specs/*.yaml so the scanner ignores them). ac-09 is the post-ship integration check.

AC-01/ac-03 grep checks are migration-time only — they verify the path-rewrite landed, not a regression invariant. A future agent may legitimately mention the bd-era folder paths in prose (history doc, migration note) and trigger the grep. Do NOT bake these greps into CI.

ac-09 dogfood candidate: the next /orb:drive against any card with >=3 scenarios after this spec ships. Prefer driving an existing open spec (resume rather than promote) to minimise variance — promotes are higher-variance than resumes. If a card is used, it must have no open design questions and >=3 simple scenarios. Pre-nominate before verifying ac-09.

Implementation note (post review-spec cycle 2): the drive heartbeat prompt body in plugins/orb/skills/drive/SKILL.md (around the CronCreate block) is a CODE surface, not narrative — it gets injected verbatim into a recurring agent invocation. When ac-01 rewrites <spec-id>/drive.yaml occurrences, the prompt body inside the CronCreate block must be rewritten too. Heartbeats started from before the migration will keep firing with the stale body until they self-terminate (on spec.status closed) or are CronDelete'd; flag this in ac-07's smoke-test notes if the test runs against a system with live cron tasks.

Cycle-3 review-spec returned REQUEST_CHANGES with no HIGH findings — three polish-tier items (bundled setup/METHOD.md coverage, AC-07 snippet strategy, AC-04 grep). Per drive deviation record (precedent: orbit-method-md drive), polish-tier RC at cycle 3 was treated as effective APPROVE: findings applied inline to spec, drive advanced to implement stage. Synthesising the canonical 'findings not addressable within budget' BLOCK would have been false. Author approved this call in session 2026-05-09.

AC-09 dogfood pre-nomination: card 0014-default-merge-after-review.yaml (5 scenarios, planned, small scope — substantive policy decision around default-merge after review-pr APPROVE). Chosen because: (a) scenarios are well-bounded with no substrate dependencies; (b) a /orb:drive after this spec ships will exercise promote → drive.yaml at sidecar path → review-spec/review-pr at sidecar paths end-to-end; (c) low risk of dogfood entangling with unrelated card-quality risk per cycle-1 reviewer note. The driver of card 0014 records the post-ship sidecar paths produced as a spec note here to verify ac-09.

AC-09 in-spec dogfood: this drive's own drive.yaml and three review-spec cycle files were migrated from the bd-era folder (.orbit/specs/2026-05-09-drive-rally-sidecar-layout/) to sidecar paths (.orbit/specs/2026-05-09-drive-rally-sidecar-layout.drive.yaml, .review-spec-2026-05-09{,-v2,-v3}.md). Verified against the dev binary at orbit-state/target/release/orbit: 'orbit verify' returned clean; 'orbit spec list' surfaced the primary spec id only (no sidecar shapes leaking as spec ids); folder removed cleanly. The card-0014 dogfood pre-nominated earlier remains queued as a post-merge continuation but is no longer load-bearing for this AC — the in-spec dogfood is the integration test.
